| Regular road (flat) | 10% | 1,450 | 0.15 | ~218 |

**4,000**

**Intersection left turns** across ~100+ cities/towns: ~350

- Highways dominate the route but contribute relatively few >45° left curves
- The Myanmar segment (Chin Hills / Shan State) is the turn-intensive crux — notorious switchback roads with hairpins every few hundred meters
- Iran's Zagros mountain crossings add a secondary cluster of winding road

**Lisbon → Spain → France → Balkans/Turkey → Iran → Pakistan → India → Myanmar → Thailand → Malaysia → Singapore**

## Estimation by Segment

| Segment | Distance | Terrain | Left turns/km | Left turns |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Iberia + France | ~2,500 km | Flat highway | ~0.01 | ~25 |
| Balkans → W. Turkey | ~2,000 km | Hilly highway | ~0.04 | ~80 |
| E. Turkey (mountains) | ~800 km | Mountain/switchback | ~0.40 | ~320 |
| Iran (Zagros + plateau) | ~2,000 km | Mixed mountain/flat | ~0.12 | ~240 |
| Pakistan/India | ~3,500 km | Mostly flat highway | ~0.05 | ~175 |
| **Myanmar (key segment)** | ~1,200 km | Extremely mountainous | ~0.50 | ~600 |
| Thailand → Singapore | ~2,500 km | Flat highway | ~0.03 | ~75 |
| Urban/intersection turns | All | Cities/route changes | — | ~250 |

## Key Reasoning

- **Highways** (majority of route): >45° single-curve direction changes are rare (~1 per 50-100 km, half being left)
- **Switchback mountain roads** (eastern Turkey, Myanmar): each switchback is ~180°, alternating left/right; ~1-2 left turns per km
- **Myanmar is the dominant contributor** — roads through Chin/Shan states are among the most winding in the world
- Intersection left turns add ~250 across ~50-100 cities/towns navigated

## My Estimate

**1,800**

This reflects an average of ~0.12 significant left turns per kilometer across the full route, driven primarily by the heavily mountainous segments in eastern Turkey and Myanmar. The uncertainty is substantial (I could see anywhere from 1,200 to 3,000 being defensible), but 1,800 is my honest best point estimate.
